Up Next

A French Olympic diver suffered an unfortunate mishap in front of PresidentEmmanuel Macronwhen he slipped and fell off a board during an exhibition event at the opening of the country’s newOlympicAquatics Centre.

Alexis Jandard was one of three divers selected to perform the synchronised plunge as part of an inauguration ceremony at the aquatics complex on the outskirts ofParis.

Jandard’s effort on the three-metre springboard, however, went horribly wrong when he slipped on the board after taking his first jump.

As the other two divers took off to complete the dive, Jandard landed awkwardly on his back before falling headfirst into the water.

The 26-year-old, who finished 16th at the Tokyo Olympics in 2021, did not sustain any serious injuries from the fall but saw the funny side as he posted a picture on Instagram showcasing red scrapes down his back from the fall.

‘Have fun, now’s the time to take the mickey because I deserve it big time,’ he said in a video on social media.

‘I want to reassure everyone. I didn’t hurt myself. There was a bit of blood, but it’s not serious. It’s not going to stop me training this afternoon.’

Speaking to French outletRMC Sport,the diver added: ‘I’ve broken boards before, but the board had never broken me before. Now it has.

‘I would have preferred this ceremony to have gone well from start to finish. There was that little hitch. If it made people smile a little, so much the better.

‘We know our sport. We know what it entails. We know that these things can happen. There is no such thing as zero risk.

‘It had to happen in front of the President and the whole of France. I think we chose the right moment.’

title: French diver falls off board after slip during Olympic inauguration dive | Metro News
description: A French Olympic diver suffered an unfortunate mishap in front of President Emmanuel Macron when he slipped and fell off a board.
